summary: Excellent audience recording of the legendary 4/6/82 Philadelphia show, highlighted by a scorching Shakedown‑Lost Sailor‑Terrapin suite and sparkling Terrapin Station.
review: The performance is widely praised as one of the great ’80s shows – the second set explodes with Shakedown into Lost Sailor, Saint of Circumstance and Terrapin Station, plus a rare Morning Dew and an electrifying The Other One.  Although it’s an audience (AUD) tape, listeners note surprisingly clear, full‑bodied sound despite occasional taper glitches.

ai_rating.rationale: Over 30 reviews, the vast majority are 4–5 stars and repeatedly highlight both stellar musical moments and a surprisingly good audience recording; only one low‑rating comment concerns sound quality.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Many reviewers describe the tape as ‘crystal clear’, ‘rich, full sound’ and better than some SBDs.  A few note low‑level distortion or uneven levels, but overall the AUD is listenable and captures the band’s energy.

standout_songs: Shakedown > Lost Sailor > Saint of Circumstance > Terrapin Station
show_quality.setlist_flow: High‑energy east‑coast show with a tight, classic first set opener (Cold Rain & Snow) and an unforgettable second‑set suite that builds from Shakedown through Terrapin Station, delivering rare moments like Morning Dew.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Praised for ‘delicious lead guitar’, perfect tone, and tight interplay with Brent on the Shakedown suite.
band_member_comments.Phil: Described as ‘bombing’ and standing tall during The Other One; his bass work is highlighted as a key driver of the show’s power.
band_member_comments.Keys: Brent’s piano work receives strong accolades – “Love Brent in piano mode!” and noted for seamless dialogue with Jerry.
